trajectory.description: Observation: Starting fresh. Analysis: This is FMoW (Functional Map of the World) from WILDS - a satellite image classification task with temporal/geographic distribution shift. The worst-region accuracy metric means I need to balance performance across all 5 regions. CLIP ViT-L/14 is pre-downloaded and should provide excellent features for this task. Decision: Write train.py using CLIP ViT-L/14 with a classification head, use multi-GPU training with DataParallel, add augmentation, and do a quick 3-epoch run to validate the pipeline and get a baseline score.

trajectory.description: Observation: 3 epochs completed with val_acc=0.1647. NaN train_loss suggests AMP float16 instability with CLIP. Accuracy is improving (2.5%→20%) showing learning is happening. Analysis: Need to fix NaN loss (switch to bfloat16 or disable AMP), and train for more epochs. The model is learning but slowly. Decision: Submit eval to get baseline score, then fix NaN issue and train longer with better settings.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline 3 epochs got worst_region_accuracy=0.3675 (Africa=36.75%, overall=53.31%). NaN train_loss from float16 AMP. Each epoch ~700s. Analysis: Need to fix NaN loss, train longer, and improve Africa region accuracy. With 9.37h remaining and ~700s/epoch, I can do ~45 more epochs total. Decision: Fix AMP (use bfloat16), train 15 epochs with cosine LR schedule, label smoothing, and region-balanced sampling. Also increase backbone LR slightly since model is learning well.

trajectory.description: Observation: Model stuck at ~17% val_acc (broken metric) but eval showed 53% overall after 3 epochs. NaN losses throughout training. Analysis: bfloat16 doesn't need GradScaler - using scaler with bfloat16 causes NaN. Mixup also adds instability. The val_acc in training is wrong (eval shows 53% but training shows 17%). Need to completely fix training: remove scaler, remove mixup, fix val computation. Decision: Rewrite train.py properly - no scaler for bfloat16, no mixup, proper LR schedule, resume from best checkpoint. Also add class-balanced sampling to improve worst-region accuracy.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training completely diverged - loss grew from 3 to 110 linearly, all batches NaN by end. Best checkpoint is still from iter 2 (worst_region=0.3675). Analysis: The checkpoint from iter 2 had NaN weights (training was broken with float16+GradScaler). Resuming from broken weights + wrong AMP config caused catastrophic divergence. Need to: 1) Start fresh from CLIP pretrained weights (no resume), 2) Use float32 (no AMP) to avoid all NaN issues, 3) Use very conservative LR for backbone (1e-6), 4) Freeze backbone initially and only train head for first few epochs. Decision: Rewrite train.py completely - start from scratch CLIP weights, float32, freeze backbone for first 3 epochs then unfreeze with tiny LR, use cosine schedule.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training has been broken for multiple iterations with NaN losses. Best checkpoint is from iter 2 (worst_region=0.3675). Only 1.58h remaining. Analysis: The iter 2 training worked with float16+GradScaler for 3 epochs. Need to replicate that success but with better settings. The key issue is that resuming from broken checkpoints and using wrong AMP configs caused divergence. Decision: Write a clean train.py that: 1) Uses float16 AMP with GradScaler (what worked), 2) Very small LR (backbone=1e-5, head=1e-3), 3) Fresh CLIP weights, 4) Freeze backbone for 2 epochs then unfreeze, 5) Run 5 epochs and submit eval. With 1.5h left, I need to be fast.
